_disclaimer: Educational decision-support data only. Not a substitute for a pharmacist or physician. MedSentry always surfaces this disclaimer alongside any flag. This list is intentionally small and curated for a demo — a production deployment should call a licensed clinical interaction API (e.g. a hospital formulary service) instead of this static file.

pairs.action: Do not combine without explicit physician supervision and monitoring.
Avoid NSAIDs; suggest acetaminophen and confirm with prescriber.
Periodic potassium monitoring recommended by prescriber.

pairs.mechanism: NSAIDs blunt ACE-inhibitor effect and reduce renal blood flow.
pairs.risk: Reduced blood-pressure control; kidney strain, especially with a diuretic on board ("triple whammy").
pairs.action: Prefer acetaminophen for pain; flag to prescriber if NSAID needed regularly.
Dose cap or alternate statin usually required — confirm with prescriber.

pairs.mechanism: Contrast-related renal impairment can reduce metformin clearance.
pairs.risk: Lactic acidosis risk around imaging procedures with IV contrast.
pairs.action: Hold metformin around contrast imaging per radiology/prescriber protocol.
Never combine; requires washout period between the two under medical supervision.

pairs.mechanism: SSRIs impair platelet serotonin uptake; additive with NSAID GI effect.
pairs.action: Consider acetaminophen instead; discuss with prescriber if NSAID is needed.

pairs.mechanism: Calcium binds levothyroxine in the gut, reducing absorption.
pairs.action: Separate dosing by at least 4 hours.

pairs.mechanism: Amiodarone raises digoxin levels via reduced clearance.
pairs.action: Digoxin dose often needs reduction — prescriber must adjust and monitor levels.

pairs.mechanism: Additive central nervous system and respiratory depression.
pairs.risk: Profound sedation, slowed/stopped breathing — an FDA boxed-warning combination.
pairs.action: Avoid combining unless a prescriber has explicitly co-managed both with monitoring.

pairs.mechanism: Omeprazole (strong CYP2C19 inhibitor) reduces clopidogrel activation.
pairs.action: Consider pantoprazole as an alternative PPI; confirm with prescriber.
Avoid alcohol during treatment and for 48 hours after the last dose.

pairs.risk: Elevated statin blood levels; myopathy risk.
pairs.action: Avoid grapefruit/grapefruit juice while on simvastatin.
